2605.21413.title: Teaching AI Through Benchmark Construction: QuestBench as a Course-Based Practice for Accountable Knowledge Work
2605.21413.paper_summary: As AI becomes part of everyday learning, many courses teach students to use it mainly as a productivity tool: how to prompt, search, summarize, write, code, and use tools more efficiently. We argue that AI education also needs a setting in which students learn to test AI and understand their own role in judging machine-produced knowledge. To this end, we introduce a course-based practice that teaches AI through benchmark construction, using deep research systems as a concrete example of AI-era knowledge work. Students turn disciplinary knowledge into verifiable expert-level questions, review one another's designs for ambiguity and shortcuts, and evaluate AI systems on the resulting tasks. This activity gives students direct exposure to a powerful tool while asking them to specify what a trustworthy answer would require. The produced benchmark, QuestBench, consists of 256 questions across 14 humanities and social-science domains. Evaluation on QuestBench shows that student-designed tasks reveal hidden failures in current deep research systems: across thirteen evaluated systems, the mean question-level pass rate is only 16.85%, and the best-performing system, GPT-5.5, reaches a 57.58% pass rate. The failures are educationally useful because they show how fluent, source-backed answers can still miss the right query, source, term, or evidence standard. Reflections from five student contributors suggest that benchmark construction can help students see professional knowledge not only as content AI may retrieve, but as the basis for judging AI outputs. We present QuestBench as a benchmark artifact and as a reusable classroom setting for a larger educational question: how students can remain responsible knowledge actors as AI enters learning and professional work. The dataset is available at https://huggingface.co/datasets/PKUAIWeb/QuestBench/tree/main.
2605.21413.paper_summary_zh: 隨著人工智慧成為日常學習的一部分，許多課程教導學生將其主要用作生產力工具：如何提示、搜尋、總結、寫作、編碼，以及更有效地使用工具。我們認為，人工智慧教育還需要一個環境，讓學生學會測試人工智慧並理解自己在評判機器生成知識中的角色。為此，我們介紹了一種基於課程的實踐，通過基準建設來教授人工智慧，使用深度研究系統作為人工智慧時代知識工作的具體範例。學生將學科知識轉化為可驗證的專家級問題，互相檢閱設計以查找模糊性和捷徑，並對所產生的任務評估人工智慧系統。這項活動讓學生直接接觸到一個強大的工具，同時要求他們具體說明可信答案所需的條件。所產生的基準，QuestBench，包含了14個人文和社會科學領域的256個問題。對QuestBench的評估顯示，學生設計的任務揭示了當前深度研究系統中的隱藏失敗：在評估的十三個系統中，平均問題級通過率僅為16.85%，而表現最佳的系統GPT-5.5達到57.58%的通過率。這些失敗在教育上是有用的，因為它們顯示出流利的、有來源支持的答案仍然可能錯過正確的查詢、來源、術語或證據標準。五位學生貢獻者的反思表明，基準建設可以幫助學生將專業知識視為不僅是人工智慧可能檢索的內容，而是評判人工智慧輸出的基礎。我們將QuestBench作為一個基準工件和一個可重用的課堂環境，提出一個更大的教育問題：隨著人工智慧進入學習和專業工作，學生如何能夠保持負責任的知識行為者。該數據集可在 https://huggingface.co/datasets/PKUAIWeb/QuestBench/tree/main 獲得。
2605.21413.author: Haiyang Shen et.al.
2605.21413.authors: Haiyang Shen, Jiuzheng Wang, Taian Guo, Mugeng Liu, Wenchun Jing, Chongyang Pan, Siqi Zhong, Zhiyang Chen, Weichen Bi, Yudong Han, Xiaoying Bai, Yun Ma
